Name: Exeggcute
Flavor-Text: Though it may look like it’s just a bunch of eggs, it’s a proper Pokémon. Exeggcute communicates with others of its kind via telepathy, apparently.

Name: Exeggutor
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Each of Exeggutor’s three heads is thinking different thoughts. The three don’t seem to be very interested in one another.

Name: Shroomish
Flavor-Text: It spouts poison spores from the top of its head. These spores cause pain all over if inhaled.

Name: Breloom
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
You can use this attack only if this Pokémon used Spore Ball during your last turn.
Flavor-Text: It scatters poisonous spores and throws powerful punches while its foe is hampered by inhaled spores.

Name: Tropius
Ability-Effect: All of your Pokémon that have [G] Energy attached can’t be Confused, and if they are already Confused, they recover from that Special Condition.
Flavor-Text: Bunches of delicious fruit grow around its neck. In warm areas, many ranches raise Tropius.

Name: Turtwig
Flavor-Text: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

Name: Grotle
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.

Name: Torterra
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each of your Evolution Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

Name: Burmy
Flavor-Text: If its cloak is broken in battle, it quickly remakes the cloak with materials nearby.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Mothim
Effects: If this Pokémon evolved from Burmy during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It flutters around at night and steals honey from the Combee hive.

Name: Cherubi
Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon. (Damage is not an effect.)
Flavor-Text: The deeper a Cherubi’s red, the more nutrients it has stockpiled in its body. And the sweeter and tastier its small ball!

Name: Shaymin V
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shaymin VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may heal 120 damage from each of your Benched [G] Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Karrablast
Flavor-Text: Its strange physiology reacts to electrical energy in interesting ways. The presence of a Shelmet will cause this Pokémon to evolve.

Name: Zarude V
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon V, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard V
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Charizard VSTAR
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Magmar
Flavor-Text: Magmar dispatches its prey with fire. But it regrets this habit once it realizes that it has burned its intended prey to a charred crisp.

Name: Magmortar
Effects: If Electivire is on your Bench, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: When Magmortar inhales deeply, the fire burning in its belly intensifies, rising in temperature to over 3,600 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Moltres
Effects: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 70 more damage. This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness.
Flavor-Text: It’s one of the legendary bird Pokémon. When Moltres flaps its flaming wings, they glimmer with a dazzling red glow.

Name: Entei V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Torkoal
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: It burns coal inside its shell for energy. It blows out black soot if it is endangered.

Name: Chimchar
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

Name: Monferno
Effects: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

Name: Infernape
Effects: Reveal the top 5 cards of your deck. This attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there. Then, discard those Energy cards and shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

Name: Simisear V
Effects: You may attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingler V
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for up to 5 [W] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Kingler VMAX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 5 [W] Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Staryu
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: If you visit a beach at the end of summer, you’ll be able to see groups of Staryu lighting up in a steady rhythm.

Name: Lapras
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: A smart and kindhearted Pokémon, it glides across the surface of the sea while its beautiful song echoes around it.

Name: Corphish
Flavor-Text: No matter how dirty the water in the river, it will adapt and thrive. It has a strong will to survive.

Name: Crawdaunt
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A rough customer that wildly flails its giant claws. It is said to be extremely hard to raise.

Name: Snorunt
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Piplup
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

Name: Prinplup
Flavor-Text: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.

Name: Empoleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in your discard pile and you have no cards in your hand, you may put this Pokémon onto your Bench. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Effects: This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

Name: Buizel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

Name: Floatzel
Effects: Put up to 2 Item cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.

Name: Lumineon V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Shuffle this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Manaphy
Ability-Effect: Prevent all damage done to your Benched Pokémon by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

Name: Cubchoo
Flavor-Text: When this Pokémon is in good health, its snot becomes thicker and stickier. It will smear its snot on anyone it doesn’t like.

Name: Beartic
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It swims through frigid seas, searching for prey. From its frozen breath, it forms icy fangs that are harder than steel.

Name: Eiscue
Effects: This attack does 40 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Fusion Strike Energy attached to all of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The hair on its head connects to the surface of its brain. When this Pokémon has something on its mind, its hair chills the air around it.

Name: Raichu V
Effects: If you go first, you can use this attack during your first turn. Search your deck for a [L] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may discard any amount of [L] Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Electabuzz
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: Many power plants keep Ground-type Pokémon around as a defense against Electabuzz that come seeking electricity.

Name: Electivire
Effects: If any of your Benched Magmortar have any damage counters on them, this attack does 90 more damage.
This attack does 50 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: The amount of electrical energy this Pokémon produces is proportional to the rate of its pulse. The voltage jumps while Electivire is battling.

Name: Raikou V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw a card.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shinx
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon generates electricity by contracting its muscles. Excited trembling is a sign that Shinx is generating a tremendous amount of electricity.

Name: Luxio
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: By joining its tail with that of another Luxio, this Pokémon can receive some of the other Luxio’s electricity and power up its own electric blasts.

Name: Luxray
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent’s Pokémon.
This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Luxray can see through solid objects. It will instantly spot prey trying to hide behind walls, even if the walls are thick.

Name: Pachirisu
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

Name: Clefairy
Flavor-Text: It is said that happiness will come to those who see a gathering of Clefairy dancing under a full moon.

Name: Clefable
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Choose a number of your Pokémon in play up to the number of heads. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: A timid fairy Pokémon that is rarely seen, it will run and hide the moment it senses people.

Name: Starmie
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon has an organ known as its core. The organ glows in seven colors when Starmie is unleashing its potent psychic powers.

Name: Mewtwo
Effects: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A Pokémon created by recombining Mew’s genes. It’s said to have the most savage heart among Pokémon.

Name: Granbull V
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Baltoy
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It moves while spinning around on its single foot. Some Baltoy have been seen spinning on their heads.

Name: Claydol
Effects: If you and your opponent have the same number of Benched Pokémon, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: This mysterious Pokémon started life as an ancient clay figurine made over 20,000 years ago.

Name: Duskull
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If it finds bad children who won’t listen to their parents, it will spirit them away—or so it’s said.

Name: Dusclops
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Its body is entirely hollow. When it opens its mouth, it sucks everything in as if it were a black hole.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a Special Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

Name: Chimecho
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Special Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Emitting ultrasonic cries, it floats on winds to travel great distances.

Name: Whimsicott V
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can’t attack during your opponent’s next turn.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Whimsicott VSTAR
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Pokémon Tool or Special Energy cards from their hand.

This attack does 60 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sigilyph
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Attach a number of basic Energy cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.
This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Psychic power allows these Pokémon to fly. Some say they were the guardians of an ancient city. Others say they were the guardians’ emissaries.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Since Dedenne can’t generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

Name: Mimikyu V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu VMAX
Effects: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. If you played Acerola’s Premonition from your hand during this turn, place 13 damage counters instead.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Milcery
Effects: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.

Name: Alcremie
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your turn does not end when you use Café Master.
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

Name: Hitmontop
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: It launches kicks while spinning. If it spins at high speed, it may bore its way into the ground.

Name: Nosepass
Flavor-Text: It hunts without twitching a muscle by pulling in its prey with powerful magnetism. But sometimes it pulls natural enemies in close.

Name: Trapinch
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its nest is a sloped, bowl-like pit in the desert. Once something has fallen in, there is no escape.

Name: Vibrava
Flavor-Text: The ultrasonic waves it generates by rubbing its two wings together cause severe headaches.

Name: Flygon
Effects: This attack does 50 damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon hides in the heart of sandstorms it creates and seldom appears where people can see it.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Riolu
Flavor-Text: It’s exceedingly energetic, with enough stamina to keep running all through the night. Taking it for walks can be a challenging experience.

Name: Lucario
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on this Pokémon. If you do, search your deck for a [F] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: Discard all [F] Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: It controls waves known as auras, which are powerful enough to pulverize huge rocks. It uses these waves to take down its prey.

Name: Throh
Flavor-Text: It performs throwing moves with first-rate skill. Over the course of many battles, Throh’s belt grows darker as it absorbs its wearer’s sweat.

Name: Sawk
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you see a Sawk training in the mountains in its single-minded pursuit of strength, it’s best to quietly pass by.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: They were sculpted from clay in ancient times. No one knows why, but some of them are driven to continually line up boulders.

Name: Golurk
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each card in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Artillery platforms built into the walls of ancient castles served as perches from which Golurk could fire energy beams.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The wastewater coming from factories is clean these days, so Grimer have nothing to eat. They’re said to be on the verge of extinction.

Name: Muk
Ability-Effect: The Retreat Cost of your opponent’s Poisoned Pokémon is [C] more.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused and Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Because they scatter germs everywhere, they’ve long been targeted for extermination, leading to a steep decline in their population.

Name: Sneasel
Flavor-Text: Its paws conceal sharp claws. If attacked, it suddenly extends the claws and startles its enemy.

Name: Weavile
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If either of them is heads, your opponent reveals their hand. For each heads, choose a card you find there and put it on the bottom of your opponent’s deck in any order.
Flavor-Text: They attack their quarry in packs. Prey as large as Mamoswine easily fall to the teamwork of a group of Weavile.

Name: Honchkrow V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon may have up to 4 Pokémon Tools attached to it. If it loses this Ability, discard Pokémon Tools from it until only 1 remains.
Effects: Your opponent reveals their hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: Until the end of your next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s Weakness is now [D]. (The amount of Weakness doesn’t change.)
Put 2 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like.
Flavor-Text: Exactly 108 spirits gathered to become this Pokémon. Apparently there are some ill-natured spirits in the mix.

Name: Purrloin
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It steals things from people just to amuse itself with their frustration. A rivalry exists between this Pokémon and Nickit.

Name: Liepard
Ability-Effect: You must discard a card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw 2 cards.
Flavor-Text: Don’t be fooled by its gorgeous fur and elegant figure. This is a moody and vicious Pokémon.

Name: Impidimp
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon and put it onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Through its nose, it sucks in the emanations produced by people and Pokémon when they feel annoyed. It thrives off this negative energy.

Name: Morgrem
Flavor-Text: When it gets down on all fours as if to beg for forgiveness, it’s trying to lure opponents in so that it can stab them with its spear-like hair.

Name: Grimmsnarl
Effects: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon for each [D] Energy attached to this Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: With the hair wrapped around its body helping to enhance its muscles, this Pokémon can overwhelm even Machamp.

Name: Morpeko V
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If you played Marnie’s Pride from your hand during this turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aggron V
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Aggron VMAX
Effects: Discard the top card of your opponent’s deck.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Wormadam
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: When Burmy evolved, its cloak became a part of this Pokémon’s body. The cloak is never shed.

Name: Probopass
Effects: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. This attack does 40 damage to the new Active Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 20 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: It uses three small units to catch prey and battle enemies. The main body mostly just gives orders.

Name: Heatran
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
If this Pokémon has any [R] Energy attached, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

Name: Escavalier
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 100 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon V (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: This attack also does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: They use shells they’ve stolen from Shelmet to arm and protect themselves. They’re very popular Pokémon in the Galar region.

Name: Klink
Flavor-Text: The two minigears that compose this Pokémon are closer than twins. They mesh well only with each other.

Name: Klang
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: When Klang goes all out, the minigear links up perfectly with the outer part of the big gear, and this Pokémon’s rotation speed increases sharply.

Name: Klinklang
Ability-Effect: Your Basic Pokémon take 20 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: From its spikes, it launches powerful blasts of electricity. Its red core contains an enormous amount of energy.

Name: Zamazenta V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may discard your hand and draw 5 cards. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Flygon V
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, this attack does 160 more damage, and discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gible
Flavor-Text: Gible prefers to stay in narrow holes in the sides of caves heated by geothermal energy. This way, Gible can stay warm even during a blizzard.

Name: Gabite
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon emits ultrasonic waves from a protrusion on either side of its head to probe pitch-dark caves.

Name: Garchomp
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may prevent all damage from and effects of attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon done to this Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
Flavor-Text: Garchomp makes its home in volcanic mountains. It flies through the sky as fast as a jet airplane, hunting down as much prey as it can.

Name: Axew
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, search your deck for a Haxorus and put it onto this Axew to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: These Pokémon nest in the ground and use their tusks to crush hard berries. Crushing berries is also how they test each other’s strength.

Name: Fraxure
Flavor-Text: After battle, this Pokémon carefully sharpens its tusks on river rocks. It needs to take care of its tusks—if one breaks, it will never grow back.

Name: Haxorus
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: Its resilient tusks are its pride and joy. It licks up dirt to take in the minerals it needs to keep its tusks in top condition.

Name: Druddigon
Effects: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon during their last turn, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

Name: Dracovish V
Effects: Before doing damage, discard all Pokémon Tools from your opponent’s Active Pokémon. If you discarded a Pokémon Tool in this way, this attack does 120 more damage.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Dragon Strike.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Farfetch’d
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The stalk this Pokémon carries in its wings serves as a sword to cut down opponents. In a dire situation, the stalk can also serve as food.

Name: Castform
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Move a basic Energy from this Pokémon to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

Name: Starly
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: They flock around mountains and fields, chasing after bug Pokémon. Their singing is noisy and annoying.

Name: Staravia
Flavor-Text: It lives in forests and fields. Squabbles over territory occur when flocks collide.

Name: Staraptor
Effects: Your opponent shuffles their Active Pokémon and all attached cards into their deck.
Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Staravia evolve into Staraptor, they leave the flock to live alone. They have sturdy wings.

Name: Bidoof
Flavor-Text: It constantly gnaws on logs and rocks to whittle down its front teeth. It nests alongside water.

Name: Bibarel
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand.
Effects: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: It makes its nest by damming streams with bark and mud. It is known as an industrious worker.

Name: Arceus V
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Arceus VSTAR
Ability-Effect: During your turn, you may search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can’t use more than 1 VSTAR Power in a game.)
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 basic Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon V in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
VSTAR-Rule: When your Pokémon VSTAR is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Minccino
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: The way it brushes away grime with its tail can be helpful when cleaning. But its focus on spotlessness can make cleaning more of a hassle.

Name: Cinccino
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Its body secretes oil that this Pokémon spreads over its nest as a coating to protect it from dust. Cinccino won’t tolerate even a speck of the stuff.

Name: Tornadus
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench, you may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Tornadus expels massive energy from its tail, causing severe storms. Its power is great enough to blow houses away.

Name: Hawlucha
Effects: Attach up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It drives its opponents to exhaustion with its agile maneuvers, then ends the fight with a flashy finishing move.

Name: Drampa V
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Acerola’s Premonition
Description: Your opponent reveals their hand, and you draw a card for each Trainer card you find there.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Barry
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Blunder Policy
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to uses an attack, if you flip any coins for the damage or effect of that attack, and if any of them are tails, draw 3 cards at the end of your turn.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Boss’s Orders
Description: Switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Café Master
Description: Choose up to 3 of your Benched Pokémon. For each of those Pokémon, search your deck for a different type of basic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. Your turn ends.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Cheren’s Care
Description: Put 1 of your [C] Pokémon that has any damage counters on it and all attached cards into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Choice Belt
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon V (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Cleansing Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [P] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Collapsed Stadium
Description: Each player can’t have more than 4 Benched Pokémon. If a player has 5 or more Benched Pokémon, they discard Benched Pokémon until they have 4 Pokémon on the Bench. The player who played this card discards first. If more than one effect changes the number of Benched Pokémon allowed, use the smaller number.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Cynthia’s Ambition
Description: Draw cards until you have 5 cards in your hand. If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent’s last turn, draw cards until you have 8 cards in your hand instead.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Fresh Water Set
Description: Heal 20 damage from each of your Pokémon.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Friends in Galar
Description: Draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Gloria
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon that don’t have a Rule Box and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Hunting Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [N] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Kindler
Description: You can use this card only if you discard a [R] Energy card from your hand.Look at the top 7 cards of your deck and put up to 2 of them into your hand. Shuffle the other cards back into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Magma Basin
Description: Once during each player’s turn, that player may attach a [R] Energy card from their discard pile to 1 of their Benched [R] Pokémon. If a player attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Marnie’s Pride
Description: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Pot Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to doesn’t have a Rule Box, it takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Professor’s Research
Description: Discard your hand and draw 7 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Roseanne’s Backup
Description:
Choose 1 or more:
• Shuffle a Pokémon from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle a Pokémon Tool card from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle a Stadium card from your discard pile into your deck.
• Shuffle an Energy card from your discard pile into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Team Yell’s Cheer
Description: Shuffle up to 3 in any combination of Pokémon and Supporter cards, except for Team Yell’s Cheer, from your discard pile into your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Double Turbo Energy
Description:
As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C][C] Energy.
The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 20 less damage to your opponent’s Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken.

Ability-Effect: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Zapdos V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V in play.
Effects: Before doing damage, discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Moltres V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon. You can’t use more than 1 Direflame Wings Ability each turn.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ultra Ball
Description: You can use this card only if you discard 2 other cards from your hand.Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Flareon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, [G] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: Once it has stored up enough heat, this Pokémon’s body temperature can reach up to 1,700 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Vaporeon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, [R] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: When Vaporeon’s fins begin to vibrate, it is a sign that rain will come within a few hours.

Name: Octillery
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Rapid Strike card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. You can’t use more than 1 Rapid Strike Search Ability each turn.
Flavor-Text: It traps enemies with its suction-cupped tentacles, then smashes them with its rock-hard head.

Name: Jolteon
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon has a Memory Capsule attached, [W] Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities.
Flavor-Text: If it is angered or startled, the fur all over its body bristles like sharp needles that pierce foes.

Name: Zekrom
Effects: This Pokémon also does 60 damage to itself. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: When the interior part of its tail spins like a motor, Zekrom can generate many bolts of lightning to blast its surroundings.

Name: Dusknoir
Ability-Effect: All Special Energy attached to Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) provide [C] Energy and have no other effect.
Flavor-Text: At the bidding of transmissions from the spirit world, it steals people and Pokémon away. No one knows whether it has a will of its own.

Name: Dedenne
Effects: If your opponent has exactly 1 Prize card remaining, this attack does 60 more damage, and your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Since Dedenne can’t generate as much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

Name: Alcremie
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your turn does not end when you use Café Master.
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each type of basic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

Name: Ariados
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may switch 1 of your opponent’s Benched Evolution Pokémon with their Active Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It spews threads from its mouth to catch its prey. When night falls, it leaves its web to go hunt aggressively.

Name: Houndoom
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for a Single Strike Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Single Strike Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Identifiable by its eerie howls, people a long time ago thought it was the grim reaper and feared it.

Name: Eevee
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Pokémon V, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: Thanks to its unstable genetic makeup, this special Pokémon conceals many different possible evolutions.

Name: Oranguru
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch a card from your hand with the top card of your deck.
Flavor-Text: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

Name: Boltund V
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [L] Energy cards and attach them to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 30 more damage for each [L] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sylveon V
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may search your deck for an Item card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sylveon VMAX
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Effects: Attach an Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, heal 120 damage from that Pokémon.
This attack does 30 more damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu V
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may prevent all damage done to this Mimikyu V by attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon until the end of your opponent’s next turn.
Effects: Put 3 damage counters on your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Mimikyu VMAX
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Effects: Put 4 damage counters on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. If you played Acerola’s Premonition from your hand during this turn, place 13 damage counters instead.
Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu V
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 [F] Energy cards and attach them to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Impact Blow.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu VMAX
Rarity: Trainer Gallery Holo Rare V or VMAX

VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu V
